title: list: non-atomic item/size reads race list_resize's atomic publish on a shared list (Py_SIZE / bytes_join / marshal readers)
description: On a shared list, list.append/pop -> list_resize publishes ob_item/ob_size with atomic release/relaxed stores, but many readers load them with plain non-atomic access (Py_SIZE in UNPACK_SEQUENCE, PyList_GET_ITEM in stringlib_bytes_join and marshal's w_complex_object). Concurrent read + resize is a data race. RULED A BUG by Thomas Wouters (Yhg1s) 2026-07-15.
sites: Objects/stringlib/join.h stringlib_bytes_join:63 (plain PyList_GET_ITEM read via b''.join)
Objects/listobject.c list_resize:165 (atomic release store of ob_item on append/pop)
race_kind: read-while-mutate on a shared builtin list (non-atomic reader vs atomic writer)
shared_state: the shared list's ob_item pointer / ob_size
severity: low (value-benign on aligned HW) but a genuine data race; ruled a bug by CPython
suggested_fix: make the list readers use the atomic accessors the writer already uses -- _PyList_GetItemRef / an atomic Py_SIZE load -- rather than plain PyList_GET_ITEM / Py_SIZE. Systemic: audit list (and other builtin container) read sites for non-atomic access under FT.
ruling: Thomas Wouters (Yhg1s, CPython RM), 2026-07-15: sent shared_list_race.py + notes/shared-builtin-concurrent-access.md verbatim, reply 'Yes, that's a bug.'
related: TSAN-0014 (concurrent list.sort of a shared list)
notes: Formerly SUPPRESSED as an assumed non-bug ('concurrent unsynchronized access to a shared builtin'); un-suppressed after the Yhg1s ruling. Py_SIZE, bytes_join, and marshal (TSAN-0010) are the same class with different reader call sites. Cross-check gh-116738: this is exactly the builtin-thread-safety audit's remit. API-SEARCH 2026-07-17: reframed as the reader-side residual of the KNOWN, CLOSED list-race class #129069 (writes fixed by #131882; TSan suppressions since removed), governed by the merged #142519 documented contract (list reads are atomic item reads). Not a novel 'lists race' discovery -- frame as 'these specific readers skipped the documented atomic load'. Both surfaced only via gh api.
fleet02_notes: fleet-02: _elementtree XMLParser._setevents aliases the caller's live list via PySequence_Fast and reads it unlocked -- another reader face of this shared-list class (TSAN-0022 folded)

related_issues.note: CLOSED/fixed (PR #131882 + #142957/#143019). THE list read/write race class: list_resize's memcpy/memmove writes vs concurrent reads, originally SUPPRESSED in Tools/tsan/suppressions_free_threading.txt. The fix made the WRITE path atomic (and our build's suppressions file is now empty). TSAN-0013 is the READER-side residual: specific readers (Py_SIZE in UNPACK_SEQUENCE, stringlib_bytes_join, marshal w_complex_object) still use PLAIN loads and still race list_resize's atomic publish on current main. Same class, reader half, still live.

Umbrella 'Document guarantees provided when sharing built-in types across threads' (CLOSED, ~28 doc PRs). Its list PR gh-142519 (MERGED) documents that list reads 'use atomic reads of each item' and are lock-free -- i.e. atomic item reads are the INTENDED CONTRACT. A plain (non-atomic) reader that races the atomic publish violates that documented contract, which supports these being real bugs (reader forgot the promised atomic), in an area the maintainers actively care about.
